steps.title: Set up the system
steps.content: We need to find an ordered pair $(x, y)$ that satisfies both inequalities simultaneously: $y \leq x$ AND $y \leq -x$. Since the answer choices are simple integers, we can test each one directly.

steps.title: Backsolving through choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A (1, 0):
Check $y \leq x$: is $0 \leq 1$? Yes. Check $y \leq -x$: is $0 \leq -1$? No.
Fails. Testing choice B (-1, 0):
Check $y \leq x$: is $0 \leq -1$? No.
Fails. Testing choice C (0, 1):
Check $y \leq x$: is $1 \leq 0$? No.
Fails. Testing choice D (0, -1):
Check $y \leq x$: is $-1 \leq 0$? Yes. Check $y \leq -x$: is $-1 \leq 0$? Yes.
Both conditions are satisfied. That matches choice D — D is correct.
